name: The triumphal entry
notes: Jesus rides into Jerusalem to shouts of Hosanna, in all four Gospels. Matthew, Mark, and Luke share the errand for the colt almost word for word — the village over against you, the colt tied, "The Lord hath need of him" — while John skips the errand entirely and frames the entry by the crowd that had seen Lazarus raised. Mark and Luke alone narrate the owners questioning the disciples as they loose the colt; Luke alone adds the Pharisees' protest, the stones that would cry out, and the weeping over the city (19:39-44), left unlinked. Matthew and John each anchor the scene in Zechariah's prophecy, but quote it differently — Matthew "Tell ye the daughter of Sion," John "Fear not, daughter of Sion."
